items.title: Jefferson Lewis to face court charged with Kumanjayi Little Baby's murder

children.content: Jefferson Lewis will face court for the first time this morning, with the hearing in the Alice Springs Local Court to be streamed live online.

image.altText: A mugshot of an Aboriginal man, serious expression looking directly at camera, yellow / orange pigment behind brown eyes.

items.title: As fuel costs soar, cycling advocates argue our priorities are all wrong

children.content: Bike shops in Darwin are experiencing soaring demand as more people turn to one of the oldest and simplest alternatives to cars amid skyrocketing fuel prices, but advocates say the federal government is still prioritising drivers.

image.altText: A man smiles in a bike workshop.

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby remembered across NT after 'unthinkable trauma'

children.content: The Northern Territory is continuing to grieve the short life of a five-year-old girl, with crowds in Alice Springs paying tribute by donning pink — her favourite colour.

gallery.altText: Two people standing in the water, holding one another with their eyes closed.
A group of young girls dressed in matching pink shirts, performing a parade dance.
Two men wearing pink shirts as they look on at a passing parade.

thumbnail.altText: Two people standing in the water, holding one another with their eyes closed.
A group of young girls dressed in matching pink shirts, performing a parade dance.
Two men wearing pink shirts as they look on at a passing parade.

items.title: Darwin club breaks record at Lake Argyle after man bitten by crocodile

children.content: Four people were bitten by a suspected freshwater crocodile just a day before a sold-out swimming competition was run in the same far-north Western Australia lake.

image.altText: A freshwater crocodile resting next to a lake

items.title: What is a town camp and what is life like there for Aboriginal people?

children.content: Kumanjayi Little Baby is believed to have been taken from her bed at night while visiting a town camp near Alice Springs with her mother. This is how town camps came to be.

image.altText: A drone shot of saltbush and roads in Alice Springs

items.title: Police release CCTV of 'outright looting' of Alice Springs businesses

children.content: NT Police have released CCTV footage of the "outright looting" of businesses caught up in rioting following the arrest of Jefferson Lewis for the murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

image.altText: A large group of people swarms into a service station

articles.title: After Little Baby's death, Canavan demands government stop 'running scared'

children.content: Nationals leader Matthew Canavan says after the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, the government must "move on" from its defeat at the Voice referendum and re-engage on Indigenous policy debate.

image.altText: Nationals Leader Matt Canavan gesturing while wearing a suit, with a screen behind him.

articles.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's grandfather thanks community, says time to 'let justice take its course'

children.content: The family of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs on Thursday, has thanked the community for their support as tributes flow in from across the country.

image.altText: A teddy bear among floral tributes

gallery.altText: A large group of people swarms into a service station

thumbnail.altText: A large group of people swarms into a service station

items.title: Electric vehicle tax discount to be wound back from next year

children.content: A popular tax discount meaning people leasing electric vehicles did not have to pay Fringe Benefits Tax will be wound back — although in the long term, more EV owners will be able to pay a reduced tax rate.

image.altText: People stand around a car with all its doors open with a sign in beg letters on the wall behind reading BYD.

items.title: Sydney Lord Mayor Clover Moore cancels 'globalise the intifada' forum

children.content: The event's cancellation is welcomed by groups representing the Jewish community in New South Wales.

image.altText: A woman wearing red sunglasses

items.title: How did this boy from the bush become head of the highest court in the land?

children.content: The High Court makes decisions that impact us every day. So who is Stephen Gageler, the person overseeing it all? And how did he get here from a tiny town in regional NSW?

gallery.altText: Leaning over Dennis, who is looking at a book, the chief justice smiles at the camera, a bookcase lined with books behind them.
The Chief Justice sits on  his desk before a series of bookcases lined with books.

thumbnail.altText: Leaning over Dennis, who is looking at a book, the chief justice smiles at the camera, a bookcase lined with books behind them.
The Chief Justice sits on  his desk before a series of bookcases lined with books.

items.title: World order could be 'rebuilt out of Europe', Carney says

children.content: The Canadian prime minister tells European leaders the world does not need to submit to a "more transactional and insular world".

image.altText: A close up of Mark Carney speaking at a press conference.

items.title: 'He's a big boy': Socceroos boss defends anti-Trump comments from midfielder

children.content: Australia coach Tony Popovic says it would be "wonderful" if Donald Trump was in the stadium in Seattle when the Socceroos play the United States on June 19, saying the US president's presence would motivate his team.

image.altText: US President Donald Trump holds the FIFA World Cup Trophy

items.title: Teen left behind after Vanuatu earthquake sues Commonwealth

children.content: A teenager with multiple disabilities stranded for days in Vanuatu's capital city after a deadly 2024 earthquake is taking the Australian government to the federal court for disability discrimination.

gallery.altText: A white teenage boy with short hair standing in a lounge room
A photo showing damage to the inside of a house after an earthquake
A white teenage boy standing next to a white adult man, both are looking stern

A white teenage boy standing next to a white adult man, both are looking stern

items.title: Cruise ship banned from port after three die in suspected virus outbreak

children.content: Three people have died and three people are ill after a suspected outbreak of the rodent-borne virus in the Atlantic Ocean.

image.altText: A cruise ship on the ocean

items.title: What Luai's shock NRL move could mean for Australian and NZ rugby union

children.content: The NRL's expansion has ramifications for more than the existing 17 clubs, with Australian and New Zealand rugby talent also in the crosshairs.

image.altText: A man signs a rugby league contract with an expansion team

items.title: Australia urged to start providing cooked lunches in schools

children.content: Tasmania is "leading the way" with its largely state government-funded school lunch program. Now advocates are calling for a national rollout of the scheme, but warn parents may need to chip in.

image.altText: Four lunch boxes packed with fruit, vegetable, meats and crackers.

items.title: Met Gala quiz: Guess who wore these outfits to fashion's best party

children.content: From Ariana to Zendaya, we're testing your knowledge of some of the most exceptional and extreme fashion on show at recent Met Galas.

image.altText: Doja Cat poses at the Met Gala, an annual fundraising gala, dressed as a cat.

items.title: Australia is experiencing HRT menopause patch shortages. Here's why

children.content: Supplies of some HRT patches may not return to normal until at least the end of the year. So what can patients do if they're impacted by shortages?

gallery.altText: An unidentified woman applies a patch under her shirt to her abdomen.

thumbnail.altText: An unidentified woman applies a patch under her shirt to her abdomen.

items.title: Who pays to put food on the table during a global crisis?

children.content: As the price of food and agricultural production goes up during the Iran conflict, supermarkets are being urged to absorb more of the cost.

image.altText: A supermarket fresh food aisle

items.title: Man charged with rape and guilty of family violence ordained in Tasmanian church

children.content: A man who is facing a rape charge in Queensland — which he has pleaded not guilty to — has been appointed to a leadership role in a Tasmanian Seventh-day Adventist church.

image.altText: A man with a long red beard stands with his hands clasped in a church while another man with a mic puts a hand on his shoulder.

items.title: Qld trains are running to a weekend timetable from today — what you need to know

children.content: Commuters have been warned to expect "more crowded" trains as a reduced timetable begins, with no end in sight.

image.altText: A crowded train platform

items.title: Bishop ordered to resign over relationship with priest appealing decision

children.content: One of South Australia's most senior Anglicans is stripped of his position over an undisclosed relationship with a priest he ordained, in a case described by the church as "a gross ethical failure".

image.altText: A woman wearing a black shirt with clerical collar and a man wearing a jacket and pink shirt with clerical collar

items.title: Law, order and $1b surplus form pillars of Labor's upcoming Victorian budget

children.content: The government has pledged to invest heavily in a crime crackdown and deliver a surplus in its upcoming 2026/27 state budget.

image.altText: Person getting arrested with handcuffs

items.title: Three main issues set to sway voters in Qld by-election

children.content: Residents in the north Brisbane seat of Stafford are set to cast their votes in a by-election dominated by cost-of-living concerns.

image.altText: A composite image of Fiona Hammond and Luke Richmond in front of a ballot box.

items.title: Accused underworld shooters far from trained assassins, defence argues

children.content: Lawyers for two men accused of murdering underworld figure Gavin Preston tell a jury their clients did not have the skills required to carry out a well-planned execution.

gallery.altText: Crime figure Gavin Preston looks unsmilingly into the camera as he poses for a photo.

thumbnail.altText: Crime figure Gavin Preston looks unsmilingly into the camera as he poses for a photo.

items.title: Two killed by car driving into pedestrians in Leipzig

children.content: Authorities say two people are dead after a car slammed into a crowd of people in the eastern German city.

image.altText: Police tape rolled out in front a police van in a large European city square.

items.title: Australia and Japan sign agreements on energy, defence and critical minerals

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and his Japanese counterpart Takaichi Sanae agreed to bolster cooperation on energy, defence and critical minerals, while warning against economic coercion.

image.altText: a female politician wearing a blazer at a table

items.title: The treasurer's reality check on Anthony Albanese's bold childcare plan

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has previously committed to delivering universal child care, but Treasurer Jim Chalmers has highlighted concerns about the cost.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese sits at a table talking with children in a child care class

items.title: It was a record race time. Moments later spectators were suspicious

children.content: Tasmania's top horse race was run 37 metres shorter than advertised. Officials knew the track distance was likely incorrect but proceeded anyway. This is what happened.

image.altText: Elevated view of horse racing event with crowd looking on.

items.title: Cadbury development to be of 'international significance': Mayor

children.content: Glenorchy City Council has approved plans for a $150 million "chocolate experience" to be built at the Cadbury factory site in Claremont in Hobart's northern suburbs.
